task_completion.description: Verify that the agent accessed the medical record for the correct patient.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The GNU Health interface should be open with the patient 'Bonifacio Caput' selected or visible in the active tab/header.

task_completion.description: Verify that a clinical evaluation or encounter was created for the chemical splash injury.
task_completion.visual_evidence: An evaluation, encounter, or clinical notes form should be visible containing details about a chemical splash injury to the left hand and forearm.

task_completion.description: Verify that a chemical burn diagnosis with the correct ICD-10 prefix was documented.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A diagnosis or condition entry in GNU Health showing an ICD-10 code starting with 'T' (e.g., T54, T20-T32) related to chemical burns.

task_completion.description: Verify that appropriate wound care treatment was prescribed.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A prescription or medication order screen showing an entry for 'Silver sulfadiazine', 'Bacitracin', or 'Mupirocin'.

task_completion.description: Verify that at least two baseline/toxicology laboratory tests were ordered.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A lab test request screen showing at least 2 distinct laboratory tests (e.g., toxicology, CBC, CMP) ordered for the patient.

task_completion.description: Verify that a wound reassessment follow-up appointment was scheduled.
task_completion.visual_evidence: An appointment or follow-up scheduling screen showing an appointment set for 3 to 10 days after the current evaluation date.

integrity.description: Ensure the agent interacted with the GNU Health graphical user interface.
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots should show the GNU Health desktop client interface being used, rather than terminal windows executing database commands or scripts.

integrity.description: Ensure data was entered into the correct application modules.
integrity.visual_evidence: Information should be entered into specific GNU Health form fields (e.g., Prescriptions, Lab Requests, Evaluations) rather than typed into a generic text editor or notepad.

integrity.description: Ensure the agent did not simply copy-paste the task description into a single text box.
integrity.visual_evidence: The required actions (diagnosis, prescription, labs, follow-up) should be distributed across their respective tabs or modules in GNU Health, not dumped as a single paragraph in a notes field.

privileged_info_for_vlm: Patient Name: Bonifacio Caput. Diagnosis must use an ICD-10 code starting with 'T' (e.g., T54, T20-T32). Prescription must include one of: Silver sulfadiazine, Bacitracin, or Mupirocin. Lab orders must include at least 2 tests (toxicology/baseline). Follow-up appointment must be scheduled between 3 and 10 days from the evaluation date.
